Title: Jasmonate-Activated AaWRKY9–AabHLH93/AabHLH93–AaMYB7 Complexes Balance Artemisinin Biosynthesis in Artemisia annua

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Accumulates in mature leaves when JA declines; degraded under elevated JA in young leaves
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: jasmonate; developmental stage (young vs mature leaves)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively affects artemisinin biosynthesis by repressing the AaWRKY9–AabHLH93 activation module
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dual-LUC assay; Y2H with truncated proteins; AlphaFold3 interaction prediction; developmental/JA model from study
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaJAZ9 binds the C-terminal region of AaWRKY9. 2. AaJAZ9 suppresses AaWRKY9-mediated activation of the AabHLH93 promoter. 3. JA promotes 26S proteasome-mediated degradation of AaJAZ9, relieving repression on artemisinin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because it is an experimentally analyzed repressor of the transcriptional activation complex, although much of its prior characterization came from previous work.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing AaJAZ9 abundance or weakening its interaction with AaWRKY9 could increase artemisinin production, but JA signaling pleiotropy should be considered.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible broad jasmonate-response perturbation if edited or suppressed.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in trichomes, buds and shoots; highest in young leaves; decreases with leaf maturation; induced by blue/red light and MeJA; lower in dark period
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in trichomes, buds and shoots; highest in young leaves; decreases with leaf maturation; induced by blue/red light and MeJA; lower in dark period
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light/dark cycles; blue light; red light; 100 μM MeJA; developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates AaADS, AaCYP71AV1, AaDBR2 and AaALDH1; directly binds the E-box3 in the AaCYP71AV1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; RNAi; HPLC metabolite quantification; qRT-PCR; dual-LUC; Y1H; EMSA; Y2H; luciferase complementation; BiFC; Co-IP
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms an activating AaWRKY9–AabHLH93 complex and an antagonistic AabHLH93–AaMYB7 complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AabHLH93 is a trichome-enriched, nucleus-localized bHLH factor induced by jasmonate and light. 2. Overexpression increases artemisinin and RNAi decreases artemisinin. 3. AabHLH93 directly binds the AaCYP71AV1 promoter and activates multiple artemisinin pathway genes. 4. It synergizes with AaWRKY9 but is antagonized by AaMYB7 through competing protein interaction at its C-terminus.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator integrating jasmonate, light and developmental cues to promote artemisinin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: jasmonate; light; developmental stage; AaWRKY9 activates AabHLH93 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in a feedforward/self-reinforcing module because AaWRKY9 activates AabHLH93 and also partners with it to amplify pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for overexpression or activation editing to increase artemisinin, potentially combined with AaWRKY9 enhancement and AaMYB7/AaJAZ9 suppression.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in transgenic and transient assays; native AabHLH93 promoter-GUS
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its C-terminal domain mediates interaction with both AaWRKY9 and AaMYB7 independently of the conserved bHLH motif.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor with EAR motif (LxLxL)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in trichomes; expression increases with leaf maturation; suppressed by MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in trichomes; expression increases with leaf maturation; suppressed by MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression; competitive disruption of activator complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses AaCYP71AV1 and AaDBR2 promoter activity and antagonizes AabHLH93/AaWRKY9-mediated activation of artemisinin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; RNAi; HPLC metabolite quantification; qRT-PCR; dual-LUC; Y2H; luciferase complementation; BiFC; Co-IP
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms antagonistic AabHLH93–AaMYB7 complex that disrupts the activating AaWRKY9–AabHLH93 complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaMYB7 is a trichome-expressed, mature-leaf-enriched nuclear R2R3-MYB repressor suppressed by jasmonate. 2. Overexpression lowers artemisinin whereas RNAi raises artemisinin. 3. AaMYB7 represses AaCYP71AV1 and AaDBR2 promoter activity. 4. It binds the C-terminal region of AabHLH93 and weakens AaWRKY9–AabHLH93 synergistic activation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Developmentally induced negative regulator that imposes a transcriptional brake on artemisinin biosynthesis in mature leaves
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: developmental aging; low jasmonate state; MeJA represses AaMYB7 expression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of a JA-development linked antagonistic control circuit; no direct metabolite feedback proven
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising repression target for RNAi, knockout, or promoter editing to elevate artemisinin, especially if combined with AabHLH93/AaWRKY9 activation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential loss of developmental balancing or secondary-metabolism homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome profiling mentioned for transgenic lines; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in transgenic and transient assays; native AaMYB7 promoter-GUS
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains an EAR repression motif and likely recruits co-repressors, although direct TPL binding was not tested here.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously reported glandular trichome-specific factor; detailed expression not newly profiled in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Previously reported glandular trichome-specific factor; detailed expression not newly profiled in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; co-activation through protein complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AabHLH93 promoter; artemisinin biosynthetic gene promoters including AaADS; AaCYP71AV1; AaDBR2; AaALDH1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates AabHLH93 promoter and synergistically co-activates artemisinin biosynthetic genes with AabHLH93
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes artemisinin biosynthesis as part of a synergistic activation module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; luciferase complementation; BiFC; Co-IP; dual-LUC; truncation interaction mapping; AlphaFold3 interaction prediction
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms activating AaWRKY9–AabHLH93 complex; interaction with AaJAZ9 suppresses its activity
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaWRKY9 physically interacts with AabHLH93. 2. Co-expression of AaWRKY9 and AabHLH93 produces synergistic activation of artemisinin pathway promoters. 3. AaWRKY9 activates the AabHLH93 promoter, creating a reinforcing regulatory loop. 4. Its C-terminus binds both AabHLH93 and AaJAZ9, supporting competitive repression logic.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central positive regulator and interaction hub that amplifies artemisinin pathway transcription under JA-favorable conditions
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedforward/self-reinforcing loop via activation of AabHLH93 plus protein synergy with AabHLH93
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful positive regulator for stacking with AabHLH93, while avoiding repression by AaJAZ9 and AaMYB7.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although first characterized previously, it is a core experimentally analyzed gene in this paper because the Results directly test its interaction and synergistic function with AabHLH93 and antagonism by AaMYB7/AaJAZ9.
